: Samples mixed with KBr and analyzed (400-4000 cm⁻¹, 100 scans).

Synthesis and Comparison of Chemical Changes Using FTIR for Magnesium Substituted Hydroxyapatite

to find the most suitable composition.
showed the most significant chemical changes (p=0.001) and improved bioactivity.

had the best chemical changes and significant FTIR peaks.

Synthesis and Comparison of Microstructural Changes using SEM for Magnesium Substituted Hydroxyapatite
-Kovács, Teodóra, Levente Studnicka, Annamária Kincses, Gabriella Spengler, Mónika Molnár, Mária
, István Endre Lukács, Imre M. Szilágyi, and György
. 2018. “Synthesis and Characterization of Sr and Mg-Doped Hydroxyapatite by a Simple Precipitation Method.”
Hydroxyapatite (HA) is commonly used in bone repair due to its biocompatibility, and magnesium (Mg) substitution in HA enhances its bioactivity, with varying effects on crystallinity and structure.
This research compares different percentages (1%, 5%, and 10%) of Mg-substituted HA, focusing on their microstructural changes using X-ray diffraction (XRD).
The study aims to determine the most effective Mg substitution level for biomedical implants by analyzing the crystallographic properties of the material.
: SEM revealed porous micrometric particles with nanoparticles. Crystallite sizes were 24 nm for HA and 18 nm for Mg-
: Significant microstructural differences and a Ca/P ratio of 1.71, close to the theoretical value of 1.67.
: 10% Mg-substituted hydroxyapatite shows the best microstructural changes with significant results (p=0.004).
improved crystallinity and bioactivity, making it ideal for medical implants.
outperforms other concentrations, making it the best candidate for medical implants.
: It shows great potential for enhancing bioactivity in bone regeneration therapies.
was synthesized by adding varying concentrations (0%, 1%, 5%, 10%) of magnesium to calcium nitrate, followed by precipitation, drying, and heat treatment at 1100°C.
: SEM was used for surface morphology, XRD for crystallography, and FTIR for spectroscopic analysis.
: Sample size (N=4) was calculated using Clinicalc.com with 80% power and 95% confidence interval.

Synthesis and Comparison of Crystallography Changes using XRD for Magnesium Substituted Hydroxyapatite
Synthesis and comparison of surface properties using Contact angle method for magnesium Substituted hydroxyapatite
is a key material in bone restoration, resembling human bone with high biocompatibility.
enhances bioactivity and bone formation by interacting with osteoblasts and modifying crystallinity.
to determine the optimal substitution for bone regeneration
10% magnesium-substituted hydroxyapatite showed significant surface changes (p=0.016, p<0.05).
Magnesium weight in hydroxyapatite was not significant (p=0.177).
improved bioactivity and wettability for bone regeneration.
demonstrated the best surface properties and porosity, making it ideal for bone regeneration and implant applications
method was used to synthesize the samples, with
was used for surface analysis by mixing powders with
material naturally present in bones and teeth, widely used in bone regeneration due to its excellent biocompatibility.
Magnesium (Mg) substitution in HA improves its mechanical strength, antibacterial properties, and bioactivity, making it suitable for advanced biomedical applications.
Recent studies focus on optimizing the percentage of Mg doping, with 10% Mg-HA showing promising structural and biological enhancements.
10% Mg-substituted hydroxyapatite showed the most significant improvement in crystallographic properties (p = 0.004).
Mg substitution reduced crystallinity and lattice parameters, aligning with findings from previous studies.
shows promise for bone regeneration due to improved bioactivity and biocompatibility.

: Calcium nitrate tetrahydrate, magnesium chloride hexahydrate, diammonium hydrogen phosphate, and ammonium hydroxide were used to synthesize pure and Mg-substituted hydroxyapatite (0%, 1%, 5%, 10%) via the chemical precipitation method.
: The solution was stirred at 600 rpm at 60°C for 4 hours, ultrasonicated, dried, pressed into tablets, and heat-treated at 1100°C for 2 hours.
: X-Ray Diffraction (XRD) using a Bruker D8 diffractometer and FTIR spectroscopy were performed to analyze the structural and chemical properties of the samples.
IBM SPSS Statistics 26 Step by Step: A Simple Guide and Reference
Kahn, Mansoor A., and Indra K. Reddy. 2000.
Kim, S. R., J. H. Lee, Y. T. Kim, D. H. Riu, S. J. Jung, Y. J. Lee, S. C. Chung, and Y. H. Kim. 2003. “Synthesis of Si, Mg Substituted Hydroxyapatites and Their Sintering

: 10% magnesium substitution in Hydroxyapatite showed the most significant crystal lattice distortion, reduced crystallinity, and formation of Mg-β-TCP (Whitlockite) phase, indicating successful Mg incorporation.
: Crystallinity differences were statistically significant (p = 0.004), confirming that Mg substitution affects HA structure, while the weight of Mg was not statistically significant (p = 0.177).
10% Mg-substituted hydroxyapatite showed the best properties.
Statistical significance (p=0.016) was found between Mg-
The weight of magnesium in hydroxyapatite was not significant (p=0.177).
Contact angle measurements and T-test confirmed differences in wettability.
